text: A coal guild offers free masks to children living beneath its smokestacks. Each mask bears the slogan CLEAN AIR STARTS WITH PERSONAL RESPONSIBILITY.

choices.text: Shut the stacks and pay workers to convert them

outcomes.text: Smoke stops. Workers rebuild the furnaces for clean heat and discover transition is easier when nobody is discarded.
Children breathe a little easier while the guild prints another success report above the smoke.
Your report concludes that soot is mostly a matter of perspective and wind direction.

text: Refinery workers bring a jar of black rain to the tavern. Management calls it locally sourced weather and has applied for a tourism award.

outcomes.text: The illness dates align perfectly with production bonuses. Management objects to the chart's hostile tone.
effects: rumor 100 Refinery records link worker illness to production targets.

choices.text: Bottle the rain as a novelty

outcomes.text: Collectors buy authentic industrial atmosphere in childproof jars.
Management offers shorter exposure to the same poison and calls it wellness.

text: A freight company diverts a toxic convoy through poor villages because the rich road has decorative hedges that might lose confidence.

choices.text: Block the convoy with every village

outcomes.text: The wagons stop. Drivers join after reading what their manifests politely call residue.

choices.text: Redirect it to the company estate

outcomes.text: The owners discover environmental urgency when the first barrel approaches their roses.

choices.text: Take payment to guide it quietly

outcomes.text: The convoy passes at night. The wells remember in daylight.

text: The marsh fish float belly-up beside a factory pipe. An inspector says the fish cannot testify because they lack standing and several organs.

choices.text: Seal the pipe with the fishing families

outcomes.text: Discharge stops. The factory pauses while the marsh begins the slower work of forgiving chemistry.
The jars gain standing before the families do, but the poison finally has an exhibit number.
effects: rumor 95 Factory waste killed the marsh fish.

outcomes.text: The first customer glows with consumer engagement.

text: Villagers build wind wheels along the ridge. The coal baron claims ownership of moving air because his smoke has occupied it longest.

choices.text: Connect the wheels as a public grid

outcomes.text: The valley lights without smoke. The baron's claim blows east and is not returned.

choices.text: License one wheel to the baron

outcomes.text: He permits clean air in exchange for naming rights to the breeze.
You become legal owner of gusts and immediate enemy of hats.

text: An old seawall protects the wealthy quarter by redirecting every storm surge into a fishing neighborhood omitted from the engineer's map.

outcomes.text: New barriers bend around homes instead of income. The sea remains difficult but less class-conscious.

choices.text: Open the wall during the next storm

outcomes.text: Water reaches the mansions. Political attention arrives moments later in waterproof boots.
The wealthy quarter stays dry. Downshore families receive a stronger apology.

text: Hospital orderlies have worked three days without sleep. The director recommends resilience training delivered by a pillow merchant.

choices.text: Close admissions until shifts are safe

outcomes.text: The city scrambles, hires more staff, and discovers exhausted care was never cheap, only unpaid.
You help for one night. The system gratefully consumes your kindness and remains hungry.
The pillows say REST EFFICIENTLY and arrive during a twelve-hour emergency.

text: Disabled patrons are building a ramp while the owner explains the historic stairs are central to the building's authentic exclusion.

outcomes.text: The entrance becomes usable. History survives the terrifying addition of more people.
A few enter by depending on your back. The stairs retain all their political power.

choices.text: Charge admission to the new ramp

outcomes.text: Access becomes available in convenient premium increments.

text: A medicine caravan can reach a plague village or a wealthy estate whose owner funded the shipment and has developed a mild decorative cough.

outcomes.text: Hundreds recover. The estate owner survives the indignity of ordinary tea.
The village saves many. The estate receives enough medicine to feel singularly valued.
One cough improves. The contract performs exactly as written and morally much worse.

text: A roadside shrine to {dead} has become an informal grief clinic. Travelers leave food, stories, and advice nobody had the courage to give while alive.

choices.text: Stay and listen without fixing anyone

outcomes.text: Nothing is solved. Several people leave less alone, which was the work available.
The shrine gains a roof, a kettle, and a schedule maintained by people who once arrived as strangers.
Grief proves marketable when carved small enough to wear.

text: An underground clinic heals any wound, then transfers the pain to an indebted stranger selected by an ancient credit score.

outcomes.text: The clinic becomes slower, ordinary, and no longer powered by invisible suffering.
Your wounds close. Somewhere, a person with bad paperwork falls to their knees.

choices.text: Redirect pain to the clinic's owners

outcomes.text: The machine calls this unauthorized empathy and begins smoking thoughtfully.

text: A storm destroys the only ferry accessible to passengers who cannot climb. The port authority proposes a memorial plaque at the top of forty new stairs.

outcomes.text: The new deck welcomes wheels, canes, carts, tired bodies, and cargo nobody has to become heroic to move.
Service returns for most people, which the authority rounds up to everyone.
Access becomes a luxury performed on someone else's shoulders.

text: A warehouse holds grain until prices rise while the queue outside grows quieter. The owner calls this patience with excellent margins.

choices.text: Open the warehouse with the queue

outcomes.text: Sacks become meals before they can become investments. Hunger loses a very profitable afternoon.
Children eat today. The owner's patience becomes more valuable tomorrow.
Your coins multiply because meals do not.

text: The cook has been stretching one stew across three unemployed streets. The tavern owner notices the missing profit before noticing the full bowls.

choices.text: Turn the kitchen into a mutual-aid table

outcomes.text: Guests bring vegetables, labor, recipes, and the radical discovery that a kitchen can have more than one owner.
The stew continues another week. Your purse becomes the temporary institution.

choices.text: Market the stew as poverty cuisine

outcomes.text: Rich diners pay extra for authenticity and leave before washing bowls.

text: A grain convoy is ordered to an army depot while drought villages along the road grind bark into flour.

choices.text: Distribute the grain village by village

outcomes.text: The convoy reaches the depot empty and the road reaches winter alive.
One village eats. The others watch the army receive the arithmetic.

choices.text: Deliver it for the military bonus

outcomes.text: The depot fills. The villages become a strategic absence on next year's map.

text: An abandoned orchard still bears fruit because generations of travelers pruned it without owning it. A duke arrives with a deed and no ladder.

outcomes.text: Every basket takes fruit and leaves seed, pruning, or labor. The deed becomes a pie liner.
People may eat on Tuesdays after signing gratitude forms.
He gains fruit, you gain gold, and the orchard gains a fence by Thursday.

text: Mushroom farmers beneath the city are charged sunlight tax despite receiving no sunlight and having filed several complaints with the sun.

choices.text: Organize the growers to refuse payment

outcomes.text: The city cannot seize a crop it cannot distinguish from the walls.
The court exempts darkness but introduces a modest shadow assessment.

choices.text: Collect tax on behalf of the sun

outcomes.text: The sun remains unaware of its revenue stream. You do not correct it.

text: The last spawning inlet can feed the coast for generations or fill one export fleet before the fish understand what happened.

choices.text: Place the inlet under fisher stewardship

outcomes.text: The fleet waits, the fish return, and restraint becomes a form of abundance nobody can warehouse.
The quota holds this year. Every merchant begins lobbying the meaning of limited.
Gold arrives immediately. The next generation of fish does not.

text: The academy locks its star charts behind a tuition gate while dockworkers navigate by scraps copied during cleaning shifts.

choices.text: Open the observatory to the city

outcomes.text: Sailors, children, cooks, and scholars redraw the sky with more practical constellations.
One navigator enters. The locked gate receives a flattering biography about opportunity.
Knowledge escapes with a price attached and immediately starts undercutting itself.

text: Laboratory assistants meet over bad cider after their director patented a cure they discovered and thanked them collectively as miscellaneous support.

outcomes.text: Clinics copy it before lawyers finish waking. Credit spreads almost as fast as treatment.
The assistants become co-owners of a cure poor patients still cannot buy.
The cure becomes valuable enough to delay releasing it.

text: A traveling school owns twelve books, one tired wagon, and a license valid everywhere except places containing children.

choices.text: Join the school and ignore the license

outcomes.text: Lessons appear in barns, camps, kitchens, and the margins of wanted notices.
One town learns officially. The wagon waits outside every other border.
Your shelf improves as the school's curriculum develops a precise rectangular hole.

text: A generations-long field notebook records how villagers restored a dying forest. The academy rejected it because several authors were grandmothers without departments.

choices.text: Credit every contributor and copy it freely

outcomes.text: The method spreads with names attached. Expertise grows roots outside the academy walls.

choices.text: Submit it under your own name

outcomes.text: The academy applauds your original discovery of people it never asked.

choices.text: Keep it local to prevent misuse

outcomes.text: The forest heals here. Elsewhere, people begin the same mistakes without the notebook.

text: The summit observatory detects a wandering world. Its owners want to name it after a donor who believes orbit is a luxury tax.

choices.text: Let the observatory staff choose together

outcomes.text: They name it Common Light, because everyone saw it and nobody put it there.
The planet receives a surname and the telescope receives a much-needed roof.

choices.text: Name it after the first cleaner to notice

outcomes.text: The cleaner becomes immortal in astronomy and still has a shift tomorrow.

text: The wreck's library contains cures, clean engines, and farming methods for a thousand worlds. Its license permits use only by the vanished species that wrote it.

outcomes.text: Knowledge crosses species, borders, and several furious legal warnings.
Clinics change overnight. Clean engines remain behind a rule enforced by nobody alive.
Humanity reaches the future through a turnstile you happen to own.

text: A recovered fabricator can make unlimited bread. The city council installs a coin slot to protect bakers from the dangerous possibility of nobody being hungry.

choices.text: Place it under baker and eater control

outcomes.text: Bakers make better things than repetitive loaves. Everyone eats while craft survives the death of desperation.
Nobody starves today. The coin slot becomes a small institution with large ambitions.
Infinity becomes scarce by appointment.

text: A future machine can clean every mug instantly. The tavern staff ask who owns the hours it gives back and why the owner is already selling them.

choices.text: Shorten every shift at full pay

outcomes.text: Workers go home early. Productivity survives without consuming the time it created.

choices.text: Expand service with the saved time

outcomes.text: More customers drink. Staff discover efficiency means carrying different mugs at the same speed.
Costs fall. The machine works flawlessly under the gaze of everyone it made poor.

text: A self-repairing road has no maintenance cost. The toll company raises prices to compensate for losing excuses.

outcomes.text: Travel becomes free. The road continues repairing itself without executive supervision.
Most travelers pay less. The company keeps ownership of something that needs nothing from it.
You earn money while the road performs all known labor.

text: A floating machine turns seawater into fresh water forever. Three captains immediately draw property lines around the horizon.

choices.text: Anchor it as an open harbor commons

outcomes.text: Every vessel fills its tanks. The horizon declines to sign the captains' deeds.
Nobody dies of thirst. Ordinary thirst becomes a profitable category.
You sell ocean in smaller containers with a picture of freedom on each label.

text: An ancient city solved hunger, housing, and medicine before collapsing over who could charge admission to the machines that solved them.

choices.text: Study the collapse before activating anything

outcomes.text: The ruins explain that abundance without shared power merely gives gatekeepers larger gates.

choices.text: Activate the machines as public property

outcomes.text: Food and medicine emerge beside warning plaques written by the last hungry billionaires.
The ruins welcome you as interim executive and ask whether collapse should resume quarterly.

text: The ship's final program offers perfect abundance if one administrator receives permanent authority to define reasonable need.

outcomes.text: The program becomes slower, noisier, accountable, and unable to quietly redefine a person as unreasonable.
Everything is provided efficiently. Temporary becomes a setting nobody remembers the password to change.

choices.text: Destroy it rather than risk hierarchy

outcomes.text: Nobody rules the machine. Nobody eats from it either. Freedom survives as a difficult empty room.
